# V7 Proof Layer Added

This version adds `24_PROOF_OR_CLOSEST_EVIDENCE_LAYER/`, focused on mathematical proof, statistical verification, and empirical evidence.

New additions include:

- Formal propositions showing why manufactured traction works under monotone ranking, feedback, social proof, recommender, review, attribution, and royalty-pool assumptions.
- Empirical evidence maps tying claims to RCTs, structural models, platform studies, and official enforcement sources.
- Counterfactual proof designs: randomized exposure, synthetic control, interrupted time series, difference-in-differences, null models, and holdout lift.
- Reproducible toy models with generated CSV outputs and figures.
- Statistical toolkit for coordination graphs, burst detection, entropy, similarity, and Bayesian evidence scoring.

Core proof line:

> If allocation depends on a proxy, and the proxy can be artificially shifted, then allocation can be artificially shifted whenever the proxy change crosses a decision boundary. Feedback and social proof can multiply the effect.
